op.name: geterr
op.notes: Get current way of handling floating-point errors. Not supported.
op.summary: Get the current way of handling floating-point errors.

blocks.text: Get the current way of handling floating-point errors.

desc_blocks.text:
A dictionary with keys "divide", "over", "under", and "invalid",
whose values are from the strings "ignore", "print", "log", "warn",
"raise", and "call". The keys represent possible floating-point
exceptions, and the values define how these exceptions are handled.

blocks.text:
For complete documentation of the types of floating-point exceptions and
treatment options, see `seterr`.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.geterr()
{'divide': 'warn', 'over': 'warn', 'under': 'ignore', 'invalid': 'warn'}
>>> np.arange(3.) / np.arange(3.)  # doctest: +SKIP
array([nan,  1.,  1.])
RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in divide

>>> oldsettings = np.seterr(all='warn', invalid='raise')
>>> np.geterr()
{'divide': 'warn', 'over': 'warn', 'under': 'warn', 'invalid': 'raise'}
>>> np.arange(3.) / np.arange(3.)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
FloatingPointError: invalid value encountered in divide
>>> oldsettings = np.seterr(**oldsettings)  # restore original
